title: Auditing a quality filter
text: sim_occ() simulates records river basins. Elevation carries basin-level random effect; temperature precipitation follow elevation; coordinate uncertainty generated depend elevation , quality environment coupled terrain, real data. ships four filters, whole point object get different verdicts.
code: d <- sim_occ(n = 1500, seed = 7) str(d[, c("bio1", "bio12", "elev", "basin", "coord_uncertainty")]) #> 'data.frame':    1500 obs. of  5 variables: #>  $ bio1             : num  15.2 15.8 20.4 15.5 13.8 ... #>  $ bio12            : num  776 850 593 701 885 ... #>  $ elev             : num  522.3 503.3 -47.8 389.8 492.7 ... #>  $ basin            : Factor w/ 20 levels "B01","B02","B03",..: 10 19 7 2 15 8 3 8 15 8 ... #>  $ coord_uncertainty: num  67 83 1417 7 52 ... colMeans(d[, c("keep_random", "keep_precision", "keep_outlier", "keep_strict")]) #>    keep_random keep_precision   keep_outlier    keep_strict  #>      0.5426667      0.5553333      0.9000000      0.2586667

title: Auditing a quality filter
text: keep_random drops records random. negative control: audit flags firing indiscriminately worthless. env-AUC sits 0.5. Retention carries environmental information, filtering reweighting point way, filtering decision carrying result. One caveat, printed advice reason: null failure resolve structure, proof absence. spatial blocks cross-validation null nothing .
code: env <- c("bio1", "bio12", "elev")  audit(d, quality = "keep_random", env = env, spatial_block = "basin") #> Warning: The environmental propensity model is anti-predictive out of fold (AUC #> = 0.442). That is a sign of misspecification, not of a benign filter: the #> fitted P(r = 1 | e) and therefore ipw_weights() cannot be trusted here. Try #> control = audit_control(quadratic = TRUE), or reduce the number of #> environmental predictors (pca = TRUE). #>  #> <sdm_audit>  #>   1,500 records, 814 retained (54.3%) by `keep_random` #>   energy distance   0.777   (permutation p = 0.958, 999 perms) #>   propensity env-AUC 0.442  (blocked by `basin` cross-validation) #>   IPW effective n    813    (99.9% of retained records) #>   verdict: BENIGN #>  #>   summary() for the reportable version.

title: Auditing a quality filter
text: keep_precision drops records coarse coordinates — coordinate precision tracks elevation. ordinary case: filter defined metadata projects onto environment without anyone intending . Read order. axes moved. retained records sit higher, colder wetter. fact species; fact coordinate can pinned . niche move . energy distance rejects null retained records random environmental subsample. Environmental, provenance? metadata buys . metadata model recovers retention well, unsurprising — filter metadata rule. number matters incremental one: environmental structure survives controlling collected record . vanished, shift provenance artefact. vanish. Can undone? effective sample size inverse-propensity weights healthy fraction retained records, reweighting supported data .
code: a <- audit(d,   quality = "keep_precision", env = env,   metadata = c("year", "source"), spatial_block = "basin" )  summary(a) #>  #> Audit of quality filter `keep_precision` #> ------------------------------------------------------------------  #> 1,500 records with complete environment; 833 retained (55.5%).  #>  #> 1. Which axes moved #>    elev             smd +0.542   KS 0.544   log-var-ratio -0.001  * #>    bio1             smd -0.506   KS 0.491   log-var-ratio +0.003  * #>    bio12            smd +0.486   KS 0.481   log-var-ratio -0.003  * #>    (* |smd| >= 0.10. Positive smd: retained records sit HIGHER on that axis.) #>  #> 2. Did the niche move at all #>    energy distance 122.663, permutation p = <= 0.001 (999 permutations, n = 833 per sample) #>    NOTE: p is at the resolution floor of the permutation test. Raise `n_perm` #>          before reporting this number, or you are reporting the test, not the data. #>  #> 3. Is the structure environmental, or is it provenance #>    env-AUC          0.860   (blocked by `basin` CV; 0.5 = retention is environmentally unstructured) #>    metadata-AUC     0.619   (usually high: the filter was DEFINED on metadata; #>                             this is a sanity check, not a finding) #>    env + metadata   0.895 #>    incremental env  +0.276  (environmental structure surviving provenance) #>  #> 4. Can it be undone #>    IPW effective sample size 570 = 68.5% of the retained records #>    the same, without trimming:  415 = 49.8% #>    lowest retention probability among retained records: 0.03895 #> ------------------------------------------------------------------  #> VERDICT: STRUCTURED #>   The filter moved the niche, and positivity supports undoing it. #>   Fit both ends of the bracket -- the filtered model, and the #>   same model with ipw_weights() -- and report the spread. They #>   move the niche in opposite directions and which end is correct #>   depends on whether coordinate error is directed, which your #>   data cannot tell you. A conclusion that survives the whole #>   bracket is safe; one that does not is a choice you made, not a #>   result you found. #> ------------------------------------------------------------------  #> These thresholds are conventions, not truths (see audit_control). #> Report the numbers, not only the verdict. plot(a)

title: Auditing a quality filter
text: verdict structured, advice fit corrections. disagree accident. Filtering downweights environments low-quality records concentrate; inverse-propensity weighting upweights rare retained records environments, undo selection. move niche opposite directions bracket truth . end right depends whether coordinate error directed — whether mislocated records pushed systematically toward particular environments, merely scattered — identifiable occurrence data. Fitting one set weights reporting result commits analysis one end bracket never measured. Fit . Report spread.
code: w_ipw <- ipw_weights(a) # undoes the selection w_trust <- trust_weights(a) # soft version of the filter  c(ipw_ess = attr(w_ipw, "ess"), retained = a$n_high) #>  ipw_ess retained  #>  570.447  833.000

title: Auditing a quality filter
text: keep_outlier Mahalanobis-distance screen environment: removes records sit far environmental space. Screens kind standard, look conservative. opposite conservative, almost invisible. Every standardized mean difference near zero. filter symmetric: trims tails, moves mean. audit built mean shifts report filter benign, energy test may well fail reach significance . Look variance ratio instead. retained records collapsed variance every axis. filter moved niche — shrunk , model fitted survivors underestimate environmental breadth species. propensity model catches , carries quadratic terms: linear model merely uninformative . fold anti-predictive: discriminates wrong direction, linear signal can find fold-specific noise. shift screen package tests |AUC − 0.5| rather AUC > 0.6. one-sided test looked AUC 0.42, concluded “structure”, told niche-deflating filter benign. Keep quadratic = TRUE.
code: o <- audit(d, quality = "keep_outlier", env = env, spatial_block = "basin")  o$shift[, c("variable", "smd", "log_vr")] #>   variable         smd     log_vr #> 2    bio12 -0.02276567 -0.6681470 #> 3     elev -0.01301121 -0.6517036 #> 1     bio1  0.01237728 -0.6510056 lin <- audit(d,   quality = "keep_outlier", env = env, spatial_block = "basin",   control = audit_control(quadratic = FALSE) ) #> Warning: The environmental propensity model is anti-predictive out of fold (AUC #> = 0.398). That is a sign of misspecification, not of a benign filter: the #> fitted P(r = 1 | e) and therefore ipw_weights() cannot be trusted here. Try #> control = audit_control(quadratic = TRUE), or reduce the number of #> environmental predictors (pca = TRUE).  c(linear = lin$propensity$auc_env, quadratic = o$propensity$auc_env) #>    linear quadratic  #> 0.3977086 0.7783753

Georeferencing precision depends terrain, accessibility, mapping density era record collected — vary across environmental space. records survive quality cut random environmental sample records collected. Dropping rest shifts environmental distribution model learns . Filtering cleaning. covariate-shift operation, biases fitted niche exactly way uneven survey effort — problem field taken seriously twenty years routinely corrects. good R packages decide records remove. nothing tells removing . gap package fills. title: Reliability audits for species distribution models
text: Four numbers, licenses different action. verdict one benign, structured, positivity-limited, summary() says one licenses next.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("KristianMiok/TrustworthySDM") library(TrustworthySDM)  d <- sim_occ(n = 1500, seed = 7)  a <- audit(   d,   quality       = "keep_precision",              # TRUE = the filter kept it   env           = c("bio1", "bio12", "elev"),    # environment at each record   metadata      = c("year", "source"),           # optional, but see below   spatial_block = "basin"                        # blocks the cross-validation )  summary(a) Audit of quality filter `keep_precision` ------------------------------------------------------------------ 1,500 records with complete environment; 833 retained (55.5%).  1. Which axes moved    elev             smd +0.542   KS 0.408   log-var-ratio -0.003  *    bio1             smd -0.518   KS 0.393   log-var-ratio +0.001  *    bio12            smd +0.505   KS 0.386   log-var-ratio -0.010  *  2. Did the niche move at all    energy distance 34.084, permutation p = <= 0.001  3. Is the structure environmental, or is it provenance    env-AUC          0.860   (blocked by `basin`)    metadata-AUC     0.641   (usually high: the filter was DEFINED on metadata;                             this is a sanity check, not a finding)    incremental env  +0.234  (environmental structure surviving provenance)  4. Can it be undone    IPW effective sample size 571 = 68.5% of the retained records  VERDICT: STRUCTURED ------------------------------------------------------------------

title: Reliability audits for species distribution models
text: Zero hard dependencies. stats, graphics, grDevices, utils. Székely–Rizzo energy statistic implemented base R tested numerical identity energy package, used test suite nowhere else. Nothing ever fail install. Quadratic propensity terms default, stay . filter removes environmental outliers trims tails symmetrically. moves mean, passes SMD screen. can fail reach significance energy test. linear propensity model merely miss — simulated data model anti-predictive fold 34 50 fold draws (mean AUC 0.42, range 0.25–0.62), quadratic model reached 0.78. one-sided AUC > 0.6 screen linear model therefore report benign one damaging filters . sim_occ() ships filter keep_outlier precisely quietly regressed. verdict deliberately asymmetric. Shift declared three screens fires, agree. makes audit quick raise alarm slow give reassurance. reliability audit right asymmetry — editorial choice, numbers can make different one. Thresholds conventions, truths. See audit_control(). Report numbers, verdict.

title: TrustworthySDM: Reliability Audits for Species Distribution Models Built on Imperfect Occurrence Data — TrustworthySDM-package
text: Species distribution models routinely fitted occurrence records cleaned, filtered otherwise curated. Cleaning removes records, record removal rarely random respect environment. 'TrustworthySDM' provides diagnostics measure quality filter environmental distribution training data, test whether induced shift can undone reweighting, report . package deliberately framework-agnostic: operates flat table records environmental values, needs rasters, background points ground truth, makes assumption model fitted downstream.

Defaults .summary. threshold Numeric cut-, required x numeric. keep "" (default) retains records values threshold, want uncertainty error field; "" retains , want confidence precision score. true_level level character factor vector denotes retained record.

title: Build a retention indicator from the output of an existing cleaning tool — as_quality

title: Build a retention indicator from the output of an existing cleaning tool — as_quality
text: Three inputs recognised. data frame .summary column produced CoordinateCleaner (Zizka et al., 2019) used present: TRUE means record passed every test. column can named column. logical vector Returned unchanged, validation. numeric vector Thresholded. case continuous coordinate-uncertainty field, e.g. GBIF's coordinateUncertaintyInMeters: keep = "" retains records threshold. character factor vector Compared true_level.

See as_quality() building CoordinateCleaner output continuous coordinate-uncertainty field. env Character vector environmental column names. metadata Optional character vector record-metadata column names (year, source, collection era, institution, ...). Supplying lets audit separate environmental shift provenance artefact. spatial_block Optional name grouping column (basin, grid cell, region) used block cross-validation, propensity discrimination inflated spatial autocorrelation (Roberts et al., 2017). control list audit_control().

Georeferencing precision depends terrain, accessibility, mapping density technology era, vary across environmental space. quality environmentally structured, discarding low-quality records removes non-randomly across environmental space, distribution model learns shifts. Filtering data hygiene covariate-shift operation. Writing \(r = 1\) retained record, environmental distribution retained set relates unfiltered one retention propensity, $$p_{\mathrm{high}}(e) \propto p_{\mathrm{true}}(e)\, P(r = 1 \mid e),$$ filtering neutral exactly \(P(r = 1 \mid e)\) vary environment, inverse-propensity weights \(w = 1 / P(r = 1 \mid e)\) undo selection (Horvitz & Thompson, 1952).

title: Audit a quality filter for environmental bias — audit
code: d <- sim_occ(n = 400, seed = 1)  # A filter driven by coordinate precision, where precision tracks elevation. a <- audit(   d,   quality = "keep_precision",   env = c("bio1", "bio12", "elev"),   metadata = c("year", "source"),   spatial_block = "basin",   control = audit_control(n_perm = 99, max_n = 200) ) a #>  #> <sdm_audit>  #>   400 records, 205 retained (51.2%) by `keep_precision` #>   energy distance   52.717   (permutation p = <= 0.01, 99 perms) #>   propensity env-AUC 0.881  (blocked by `basin` cross-validation) #>   IPW effective n    101    (49.5% of retained records) #>   verdict: STRUCTURED #>  #>   summary() for the reportable version. #>

title: Tuning constants for audit() — audit_control
text: thresholds conventions, truths. conventional cut-offs covariate-balance propensity literature, exposed arguments precisely right value property study, method. Report numbers, verdict.

title: Tuning constants for audit() — audit_control
code: audit_control(   smd_threshold = 0.1,   auc_threshold = 0.6,   alpha = 0.05,   ess_threshold = 0.1,   n_perm = 999L,   max_n = 1500L,   k = 5L,   quadratic = TRUE,   pca = FALSE,   pca_var = 0.95,   trim = c(0.01, 0.99),   reference = c("all", "low") )

title: Tuning constants for audit() — audit_control
text: smd_threshold Absolute standardized mean difference environmental axis counts shifted. 0.1 convention comes propensity-score balance literature (Austin, 2011). auc_threshold --fold AUC environmental propensity model retention counts environmentally structured. 0.5 structure; 0.6 deliberately low bar, reliability audit false alarm cheaper false reassurance. alpha Significance level energy-distance permutation test. ess_threshold Effective sample size, fraction retained records, inverse-propensity weighting treated positivity-limited: weights carried small number records global reweighting fragile. n_perm Permutations energy test. smallest attainable p-value 1 / (n_perm + 1); keep well alpha test report resolution floor rather evidence. max_n Common subsample size energy test, bounds \(O(n^2)\) pairwise-distance computation. k Number cross-validation folds. quadratic Include squared environmental terms propensity model. Keep : filter trims environmental outliers leaves mean untouched invisible linear propensity model. pca Reduce environmental block PCA fitting propensity model. Useful predictors numerous collinear, conservative: tend understate environmental signal. pca_var Proportion variance retained pca = TRUE. trim Quantiles inverse-propensity weights trimmed. reference Reference sample energy test: "" (contrast model fitted filtered data actually experiences) "low" (disjoint high-versus-low contrast).

title: Tuning constants for audit() — audit_control

Fit ends bracket (filtered, IPW-weighted) report spread. positivity-limited filter moved niche, reweighting carried handful records. Global IPW fragile . Stratify, report bracket without committing either end.

title: Print, summarise and plot an audit — sdm_audit_methods
code: d <- sim_occ(n = 400, seed = 1) a <- audit(d,   quality = "keep_precision", env = c("bio1", "bio12", "elev"),   metadata = "source", spatial_block = "basin",   control = audit_control(n_perm = 99, max_n = 200) ) a #>  #> <sdm_audit>  #>   400 records, 205 retained (51.2%) by `keep_precision` #>   energy distance   52.717   (permutation p = <= 0.01, 99 perms) #>   propensity env-AUC 0.881  (blocked by `basin` cross-validation) #>   IPW effective n    101    (49.5% of retained records) #>   verdict: STRUCTURED #>  #>   summary() for the reportable version. #>  summary(a) #>  #> Audit of quality filter `keep_precision` #> ------------------------------------------------------------------  #> 400 records with complete environment; 205 retained (51.2%).  #>  #> 1. Which axes moved #>    elev             smd +0.644   KS 0.614   log-var-ratio -0.129  * #>    bio1             smd -0.604   KS 0.597   log-var-ratio -0.080  * #>    bio12            smd +0.551   KS 0.505   log-var-ratio +0.026  * #>    (* |smd| >= 0.10. Positive smd: retained records sit HIGHER on that axis.) #>  #> 2. Did the niche move at all #>    energy distance 52.717, permutation p = <= 0.01 (99 permutations, n = 200 per sample) #>    NOTE: p is at the resolution floor of the permutation test. Raise `n_perm` #>          before reporting this number, or you are reporting the test, not the data. #>  #> 3. Is the structure environmental, or is it provenance #>    env-AUC          0.881   (blocked by `basin` CV; 0.5 = retention is environmentally unstructured) #>    metadata-AUC     0.554   (usually high: the filter was DEFINED on metadata; #>                             this is a sanity check, not a finding) #>    env + metadata   0.907 #>    incremental env  +0.353  (environmental structure surviving provenance) #>  #> 4. Can it be undone #>    IPW effective sample size 101 = 49.5% of the retained records #>    the same, without trimming:  82 = 40.2% #>    lowest retention probability among retained records: 0.04218 #> ------------------------------------------------------------------  #> VERDICT: STRUCTURED #>   The filter moved the niche, and positivity supports undoing it. #>   Fit both ends of the bracket -- the filtered model, and the #>   same model with ipw_weights() -- and report the spread. They #>   move the niche in opposite directions and which end is correct #>   depends on whether coordinate error is directed, which your #>   data cannot tell you. A conclusion that survives the whole #>   bracket is safe; one that does not is a choice you made, not a #>   result you found. #> ------------------------------------------------------------------  #> These thresholds are conventions, not truths (see audit_control). #> Report the numbers, not only the verdict. #>

title: Trust weights: soft downweighting instead of a hard cut — trust_weights
code: d <- sim_occ(n = 400, seed = 1) a <- audit(d,   quality = "keep_precision", env = c("bio1", "bio12", "elev"),   control = audit_control(n_perm = 99, max_n = 200) ) w <- trust_weights(a) range(w) #> [1] 0.09702734 1.93237968

title: TrustworthySDM 0.1.0
text: First release. One module: audit(). audit() diagnoses whether quality filter shifted environmental distribution set occurrence records, returns one three verdicts: benign, structured, positivity-limited. ipw_weights() trust_weights() implement two defensible corrections. move niche opposite directions, point. as_quality() bridges CoordinateCleaner output continuous coordinate-uncertainty field. sim_occ() simulates occurrence records four filters, one verdict plus symmetric outlier screen invisible mean-shift diagnostics. hard dependencies. energy statistic implemented base R tested numerical identity energy package.
